definition: A stack is a Last-In, First-Out data structure where insertion and removal happen at the top.
deepExplanation: A stack models nested or reversible work. push adds a new top item, pop removes the current top item, and peek reads the current top without removing it. Empty-stack operations must be handled to avoid underflow.

codeExamples.title: Integer stack

diagrams.caption: After pushing A, B, C, the next pop returns C.

commonMistakes: Popping from an empty stack without handling underflow.
Removing from the bottom instead of the top.
practiceQuestions: Push A, then B, then C. What does pop return?
